---
title: "per_timepoint_fisher_codon — Olive report"
subtitle: "ribostall stall_sites — enrichment stats"
date: 2026-05-21
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{needspace}
tbl-colwidths: [9, 13, 9, 20, 15, 14, 20]
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![3x3 grid of BWM-vs-control codon volcano panels: rows are timepoints (day 0 / day 5 / day 10) and columns are ribosome sites (A / P / E). Each panel plots -log10(FDR) against log2(odds ratio); triangles mark FDR<0.05 hits and circles are non-significant. Glyphs are coloured by amino-acid property of the encoded residue (acidic = red, basic = blue, hydrophobic = green, neutral = orange, polar = purple). Day-0 panels are dense with FDR<0.05 hits while day-5 and day-10 panels collapse to the origin with at most a handful of hits.](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/codon/composite/codon_fisher_composite.png){fig-pos="H" width=92%}

Each glyph is a single codon at one (timepoint, site) BH-FDR family of 61 sense codons; horizontal dashed line is FDR = 0.05 and vertical dashed lines mark +/-0.5 log2(OR). Positive log2(OR) means BWM-enriched relative to control; negative means BWM-depleted. The x-axis extends to +/-10 to accommodate rare-codon depletions whose magnitudes are dominated by the small-k floor (see `rare-codon-low-count`).

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `per_timepoint_fisher_codon.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `per_timepoint_fisher`
- **Sister CSV(s):** `per_timepoint_fisher_aa.csv`
- **Test:** Fisher's exact (two-sided), BWM vs control 2x2 contingency per (timepoint, site, codon); BH-FDR within each (timepoint, site) family of 61 sense codons
- **Design:** Per (timepoint, site, codon) 2x2 BWM-vs-control contingency tested by two-sided Fisher's exact; replicates summed before the test (pseudoreplicated); BH-FDR applied independently within each of 9 (timepoint, site) families of 61 codons (549 tests total).

### Family-level caveats

- **pseudorep** — 2 biological reps per (condition,timepoint) summed before Fisher's exact; the source script literally prints a pseudoreplication warning. Anti-conservative p-values.
- **large-N-Fisher-anticonservative** — pooled totals are in the thousands (e.g. BWM_total ~6091, control_total ~27732 at day_0); Fisher's exact returns vanishing p-values for tiny absolute deviations (odds_ratio 1.36 at p=1e-10). odds_ratio is the primary effect column, not p.
- **imbalanced-N** — BWM_total ~6091 vs control_total ~27732 at day_0 (~5x asymmetry); Fisher handles imbalance fine but downstream interpretation should not over-read odds ratios when one cell is small.
- **bh-per-(timepoint,site)** — BH-FDR is computed within each (timepoint,site) family of ~20 (aa) or ~61 (codon) tests. p_adj means "corrected within this slice", not across the whole CSV.

---

## Headline

549 tests (3 timepoints x 3 sites x 61 sense codons), 86 hits at p_adj<0.05 (15.7%), 108 at p_adj<0.10 (19.7%). Per-(timepoint, site) cell n_sig at p_adj<0.05: day_0 A=27, P=28, E=27; day_5 A=0, P=0, E=3; day_10 A=0, P=1, E=0. Of 183 (site, codon) cells: 48 are concordant in OR direction across all 3 timepoints (17 BWM-enriched, 31 BWM-depleted, with the depleted set heavily weighted toward low-count rare-codon rows that are direction-stable but per-cell magnitude-unstable); 133 show at least one sign change across the 3 timepoint values; 1 of those 133 (E:AAG high-count) has the sign change at p_adj<0.05 on both opposite-sign rows. 3 rows have p_adj < 1e-10, all at day_0 and all on AAG (Lys): day_0 A AAG log2OR=+0.654 p_adj=1.38e-17 (BWM_k=618 ctrl_k=1857), day_0 P AAG log2OR=+0.692 p_adj=1.04e-18 (BWM_k=591 ctrl_k=1729), day_0 E AAG log2OR=+0.745 p_adj=2.03e-30 (file-min, BWM_k=866 ctrl_k=2495) — these three sites get equal billing in this row of the headline. Largest-magnitude single-cell BWM-vs-control divergences at p_adj<0.05 with combined k>=50 (mixing directions, including rare-codon flagged): day_0 A AAG +0.654, day_0 A CGC +0.592 (BWM_k=104), day_0 A AGA +0.559, day_0 A CTC +0.427, day_0 P CGC +0.954 (BWM_k=95, rare-codon), day_0 P AAG +0.692, day_0 P AGA +0.579, day_0 P ATC +0.424, day_0 E CGC +0.923 (BWM_k=93, rare-codon), day_0 E AAG +0.745. Note: the file's largest absolute |log2OR| values at p_adj<0.05 (day_0 A TGT -1.843, day_0 A TTT -1.521, day_0 A AAT -1.499, day_0 A TAT -1.346, day_0 P ACA -2.005, day_0 E ACG -2.293) are all rare-codon depletions with BWM_k under 40; their direction is consistent with the per-cell counts but the magnitude is dominated by the small-k floor and they are not in the high-count concordant set. Largest-magnitude high-count concordant cells (mean log2OR across the 3 timepoints, single sign throughout, k_BWM>=100 in every timepoint): A:AGA +0.294, P:AAG +0.379, P:GAA -0.310, P:AGA +0.249. Treat per-cell n_sig differences across timepoints as power-modulated by the BWM/control N imbalance, not as direction-of-effect biology.

## Top hits

The effect column is `log2_OR` (the log2 of the `odds_ratio` column). Direction is fixed by the BWM-vs-control contingency layout: positive log2(OR) = BWM-enriched, negative = BWM-depleted. `p_value` is the raw two-sided Fisher's exact p; `p_adj` is BH-corrected within each (timepoint, site) family of 61 codons. *Inclusion criterion: rows with `p_adj` < 0.05 and combined `k_BWM + k_control` >= 50; no row cap. Each table merges all three ribosome sites for a (timepoint, direction) pair; rows are grouped by `site` in A -> P -> E order and within each site sorted by `|log2_OR|` descending. If no rows clear the threshold for a given (timepoint, direction) combination, that table is omitted entirely.* The `aa` column is the single-letter amino acid translation of each codon.

The two summary tables that follow the per-(timepoint, site) blocks (cross-timepoint direction concordance and direction-flip cells) are reproduced under "Cross-timepoint summary" at the end of this section.

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_0 — Enriched (BWM > control)

| site| codon| aa| effect (`log2_OR`)| raw p (`p_value`)| adjusted p (`p_adj`)| flag|
| ---| ---| ---| ---| ---| ---| ---|
| A| AAG| K| +0.654| 2.27e-19| 1.38e-17| iid-amp |
| A| CGC| R| +0.592| 5.34e-04| 2.03e-03| |
| A| AGA| R| +0.559| 5.91e-05| 3.68e-04| |
| A| CAC| H| +0.448| 1.14e-02| 2.67e-02| rare-codon |
| A| CTC| L| +0.427| 1.20e-03| 4.00e-03| |
| P| CGC| R| +0.954| 3.45e-07| 3.45e-06| rare-codon|
| P| AAG| K| +0.692| 1.71e-20| 1.04e-18| iid-amp |
| P| AGA| R| +0.579| 4.13e-05| 2.47e-04| |
| P| ATC| I| +0.424| 4.46e-05| 2.47e-04| |
| P| CGT| R| +0.410| 6.59e-04| 2.41e-03| |
| E| CGC| R| +0.923| 1.02e-06| 1.44e-05| rare-codon|
| E| AAG| K| +0.745| 3.33e-32| 2.03e-30| iid-amp |
| E| CTC| L| +0.539| 3.10e-05| 1.89e-04| |
| E| AGA| R| +0.468| 3.82e-04| 1.66e-03| |
| E | CGT | R | +0.465 | 9.31e-04 | 3.47e-03 | |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_0 — Depleted (BWM < control)

| site| codon| aa| effect (`log2_OR`)| raw p (`p_value`)| adjusted p (`p_adj`)| flag|
| ---| ---| ---| ---| ---| ---| ---|
| A| TGT| C| -1.843| 1.25e-04| 5.88e-04| rare-codon|
| A| TTT| F| -1.521| 4.44e-06| 5.41e-05| rare-codon|
| A| AAT| N| -1.499| 3.39e-11| 1.03e-09| rare-codon|
| A| TAT| Y| -1.346| 2.03e-09| 4.13e-08| rare-codon|
| A| AGT| S| -1.340| 8.50e-03| 2.35e-02| rare-codon|
| P| ACA| T| -2.005| 3.66e-09| 5.58e-08| rare-codon|
| P| ACG| T| -1.868| 1.32e-04| 6.17e-04| rare-codon|
| P| TGT| C| -1.477| 2.59e-04| 1.05e-03| rare-codon|
| P| TTT| F| -1.299| 2.35e-05| 1.59e-04| rare-codon|
| P| AGT| S| -1.277| 1.50e-03| 5.07e-03| rare-codon|
| E| ACG| T| -2.293| 1.24e-04| 6.32e-04| rare-codon|
| E| ACA| T| -1.555| 2.39e-06| 2.43e-05| rare-codon|
| E| CCG| P| -1.402| 2.40e-03| 7.20e-03| rare-codon|
| E| GTA| V| -1.402| 2.40e-03| 7.20e-03| rare-codon|
| E | TCA | S | -1.396 | 1.18e-06 | 1.44e-05 | rare-codon |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_5 — Enriched (BWM > control)

| site| codon| aa| effect (`log2_OR`)| raw p (`p_value`)| adjusted p (`p_adj`)| flag|
| ---| ---| ---| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| E | CCA | P | +0.358 | 1.20e-03 | 2.52e-02 | |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_5 — Depleted (BWM < control)

| site| codon| aa| effect (`log2_OR`)| raw p (`p_value`)| adjusted p (`p_adj`)| flag|
| ---| ---| ---| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| E| GAG| E| -0.300| 1.16e-04| 5.00e-03| |
| E | AAG | K | -0.226 | 1.64e-04 | 5.00e-03 | |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_10 — Enriched (BWM > control)

| site| codon| aa| effect (`log2_OR`)| raw p (`p_value`)| adjusted p (`p_adj`)| flag|
| ---| ---| ---| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| P | GTC | V | +0.563 | 6.61e-05 | 4.03e-03 | |


### Cross-timepoint summary (two ranking tables)

```{=html}
<style>
#cross-timepoint-summary-two-ranking-tables table tbody tr td,
#cross-timepoint-summary-two-ranking-tables table tbody tr th {
  padding-top: 5px;
  padding-bottom: 5px;
}
</style>
```

```{=latex}
\begingroup\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.25}
```

#### Cross-timepoint direction concordance

48 of 183 (site, codon) cells hold the same OR direction across all 3 timepoints. Below the cells are split by displayed mean-log2OR direction into BWM-enriched and BWM-depleted sub-tables; each is sorted by `#sig` descending, then by `|mean log2OR|` descending, and capped at the top 15 cells per direction (omitted-row counts in the footer beneath each sub-table). `#sig` is the count (out of 3) of timepoints where the row reaches `p_adj` < 0.05 (the FDR-corrected threshold); `min p_adj` is the smallest of the 3 per-timepoint values. The `codon` column carries the `site:codon(AA)` triple — the site letter (A/P/E), the three-letter codon, and the one-letter amino acid translation in parentheses (no space between the codon and the parenthesised AA). The `per-tp log2OR` column lists the three per-timepoint values in chronological order (day 0, day 5, day 10); `\*` marks each per-timepoint value at `p_adj` < 0.05.

##### Concordance — BWM-enriched (mean log2OR > 0)

| codon | mean log2OR | per-tp log2OR | #sig | min p_adj | flag |
| --- | --- | :---: | --- | --- | --- |
| E:CGC(R) | +0.514 | +0.92\* +0.04 +0.58 | 1/3 | 1.44e-05 | rare-codon (0, 10) |
| P:AAG(K) | +0.379 | +0.69\* +0.25 +0.20 | 1/3 | 1.04e-18 | iid-amp |
| A:AGA(R) | +0.294 | +0.56\* +0.03 +0.29 | 1/3 | 3.68e-04 | |
| A:CAC(H) | +0.292 | +0.45\* +0.20 +0.23 | 1/3 | 2.67e-02 | rare-codon (0, 10) |
| P:AGA(R) | +0.249 | +0.58\* +0.10 +0.06 | 1/3 | 2.47e-04 | |
| P:CGT(R) | +0.230 | +0.41\* +0.16 +0.12 | 1/3 | 2.41e-03 | |
| P:ATA(I) | +0.442 | +0.14 +0.39 +0.80 | 0/3 | 6.44e-01 | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |
| P:AGG(R) | +0.378 | +0.43 +0.58 +0.12 | 0/3 | 5.94e-01 | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |
| E:AGG(R) | +0.303 | +0.47 +0.42 +0.02 | 0/3 | 5.35e-01 | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |
| A:CGT(R) | +0.230 | +0.26 +0.16 +0.27 | 0/3 | 8.81e-02 | |
| P:CTT(L) | +0.204 | +0.13 +0.14 +0.34 | 0/3 | 3.75e-01 | |
| A:GGT(G) | +0.200 | +0.10 +0.07 +0.43 | 0/3 | 5.64e-01 | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |
| P:CTC(L) | +0.196 | +0.30 +0.07 +0.22 | 0/3 | 8.90e-02 | |
| A:TCC(S) | +0.196 | +0.29 +0.07 +0.22 | 0/3 | 1.43e-01 | rare-codon (0) |
| P:GCG(A) | +0.179 | +0.03 +0.17 +0.34 | 0/3 | 8.93e-01 | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |

: {tbl-colwidths="[14, 8, 30, 8, 14, 26]"}

(15 highest-ranked enriched concordant cells shown; 2 more enriched concordant cells below the cutoff (#sig, |mean log2OR|) = (0/3, 0.179) are in the data but not listed.)

##### Concordance — BWM-depleted (mean log2OR < 0)

| codon | mean log2OR | per-tp log2OR | #sig | min p_adj | flag |
| --- | --- | :---: | --- | --- | --- |
| P:GTA(V) | -0.752 | -1.06\* -0.04 -1.15 | 1/3 | 2.65e-02 | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |
| P:ACA(T) | -0.730 | -2.00\* -0.07 -0.12 | 1/3 | 5.58e-08 | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |
| E:CGA(R) | -0.721 | -1.17\* -0.01 -0.98 | 1/3 | 2.22e-02 | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |
| P:CCT(P) | -0.674 | -1.24\* -0.26 -0.52 | 1/3 | 3.48e-02 | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |
| P:CGA(R) | -0.662 | -1.21\* -0.26 -0.52 | 1/3 | 4.50e-02 | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |
| E:TCG(S) | -0.527 | -0.84\* -0.08 -0.66 | 1/3 | 1.16e-02 | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |
| A:ACA(T) | -0.523 | -1.11\* -0.15 -0.31 | 1/3 | 3.68e-04 | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |
| P:GCA(A) | -0.461 | -0.82\* -0.16 -0.41 | 1/3 | 6.64e-03 | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |
| E:AAT(N) | -0.446 | -1.11\* -0.16 -0.06 | 1/3 | 5.16e-07 | rare-codon (0, 10) |
| P:TCA(S) | -0.443 | -0.95\* -0.14 -0.25 | 1/3 | 2.41e-03 | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |
| E:TTT(F) | -0.417 | -1.11\* -0.06 -0.08 | 1/3 | 8.03e-03 | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |
| A:AAA(K) | -0.391 | -0.89\* -0.05 -0.23 | 1/3 | 4.52e-05 | rare-codon (0, 10) |
| P:GAA(E) | -0.310 | -0.69\* -0.17 -0.07 | 1/3 | 3.45e-06 | |
| A:GAA(E) | -0.242 | -0.46\* -0.24 -0.02 | 1/3 | 4.61e-04 | |
| E:GAT(D) | -0.199 | -0.42\* -0.13 -0.04 | 1/3 | 1.66e-03 | |

: {tbl-colwidths="[14, 8, 30, 8, 14, 26]"}

(15 highest-ranked depleted concordant cells shown; 16 more depleted concordant cells below the cutoff (#sig, |mean log2OR|) = (1/3, 0.199) are in the data but not listed.)

#### Direction-flip cells across timepoints

133 of 183 (site, codon) cells show at least one sign change across the 3 timepoint values. Only 1 (E:AAG) has the flip register at `p_adj` < 0.05 on both opposite-sign rows — flagged `sig-flip: yes` below. `#sig` is the count (out of 3 timepoints) of rows reaching `p_adj` < 0.05 (the FDR-corrected threshold); the `per-tp log2OR` column lists the three per-timepoint values in chronological order (day 0, day 5, day 10); `\*` marks each per-timepoint value that reaches the threshold. Sorted by `#sig` descending, then by max single-cell `|log2OR|` descending.

| codon | log2OR range | per-tp log2OR | #sig | sig-flip | flag |
| --- | --- | :---: | --- | --- | --- |
| E:AAG(K) | [-0.226, +0.745] | +0.75\* -0.23\* +0.02 | 2/3 | yes | iid-amp (0 cell only) |
| E:ACG(T) | [-2.293, +0.663] | -2.29\* +0.16 +0.66 | 1/3 | no | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |
| A:GCG(A) | [-2.165, +0.491] | -1.19\* +0.49 -2.17 | 1/3 | no | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |
| P:ACG(T) | [-1.868, +0.086] | -1.87\* +0.09 -0.95 | 1/3 | no | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |
| A:TGT(C) | [-1.843, +0.548] | -1.84\* -0.30 +0.55 | 1/3 | no | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |
| E:ACA(T) | [-1.555, +0.292] | -1.56\* +0.29 +0.04 | 1/3 | no | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |
| A:TTT(F) | [-1.521, +0.244] | -1.52\* -0.00 +0.24 | 1/3 | no | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |
| A:AAT(N) | [-1.499, +0.077] | -1.50\* -0.20 +0.08 | 1/3 | no | rare-codon (0, 10) |
| P:TGT(C) | [-1.477, +0.077] | -1.48\* -0.18 +0.08 | 1/3 | no | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |
| A:GTA(V) | [-1.418, +0.039] | -1.15\* +0.04 -1.42 | 1/3 | no | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |
| E:CCG(P) | [-1.402, +0.512] | -1.40\* +0.51 -0.58 | 1/3 | no | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |
| E:GTA(V) | [-1.402, +0.644] | -1.40\* +0.64 -0.42 | 1/3 | no | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |
| E:TCA(S) | [-1.396, +0.405] | -1.40\* -0.05 +0.40 | 1/3 | no | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |
| A:TAT(Y) | [-1.346, +0.091] | -1.35\* +0.09 -0.32 | 1/3 | no | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |
| A:AGT(S) | [-1.340, +0.092] | -1.34\* +0.06 +0.09 | 1/3 | no | rare-codon (0, 5, 10) |

: {tbl-colwidths="[14, 20, 27, 6, 6, 27]"}

(15 highest-magnitude flip cells shown; 118 more flip cells below the cutoff (#sig, max |log2OR|) = (1/3, 1.340) are in the data but not listed.)

```{=latex}
\endgroup
```

### Flag glossary

- `iid-amp` — single per-timepoint `p_adj` falls below 1e-10. The magnitude of `-log10(p_adj)` is inflated by Fisher's anti-conservative behaviour at large pooled N + correlated stall positions; rank by effect size, not by p magnitude.
- `rare-codon` — `BWM_k` < 100 or `ctrl_k` < 100 in this row (or at least one timepoint, for cross-timepoint cells). OR estimate is unstable at small k and the magnitude is dominated by the small-k floor; large |log2OR| in flagged rows may reflect sampling noise rather than biology.

## Numbers at a glance
- `n_tests`: 549 (3 timepoints x 3 sites x 61 sense codons)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 86 (15.7%)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 108 (19.7%)
- `min adjusted-p`: 2.0325194950162870e-30 (day_0 E AAG, log2OR=+0.745, BWM_k=866/n=6091, ctrl_k=2495/n=27732)
- `p_floor`: n/a — Fisher's exact has no analytic floor; effective discreteness floor at small (k_BWM + k_ctrl) is captured by the `larger-bh-family` and `rare-codon-low-count` caveats
- Cells with p_adj < 1e-10: 3 of 549 (day_0 E AAG, day_0 P AAG, day_0 A AAG); all flagged `iid-amp`
- 2 rows have OR exactly = 0 (BWM_k = 0): day_0 E ATA (ctrl_k=16, p_adj=0.152) and day_10 P TTA (ctrl_k=1, p_adj=1.0); reported with log2OR=-Inf and excluded from |mean log2OR| ranking
- Cross-(timepoint, site) BH families and their n_sig at p_adj<0.05:

| timepoint| A| P| E| BWM_total| control_total| ctrl:BWM ratio|
| ---| ---| ---| ---| ---| ---| ---|
| day_0| 27/61 (min p 1.38e-17)| 28/61 (min p 1.04e-18)| 27/61 (min p 2.03e-30)| 6091| 27732| 4.55|
| day_5| 0/61 (min p 2.83e-01)| 0/61 (min p 6.98e-02)| 3/61 (min p 4.99e-03)| 11935| 11177| 0.94|
| day_10 | 0/61 (min p 9.77e-02) | 1/61 (min p 4.03e-03) | 0/61 (min p 1.36e-01) | 6945 | 8788 | 1.27 |

- Cross-timepoint (site, codon) cells, all 3 timepoints same OR direction: 48/183 (17 BWM-enriched, 31 BWM-depleted)
- Cross-timepoint (site, codon) cells with at least one sign change: 133/183
- Cells with sign change at p_adj<0.05 on both opposite-sign rows: 1 (E:AAG)

## Biological interpretation

**Day-0 AAG (Lys) BWM-enrichment is the file's strongest concrete signal at all three ribosome sites (concrete).** AAG-at-E (+0.75 log2(OR), p_adj=2.0e-30), AAG-at-P (+0.69), and AAG-at-A (+0.65) are the only three rows in the file with p_adj < 1e-10, all at day 0, all at high count (BWM_k = 591-866). Even discounting the p magnitudes — which the `iid-amp` flag explicitly warns against over-reading — the effect sizes are large (OR ~= 1.6) and reproduced across three independent ribosome sites with consistent direction. Co-enrichment of a basic codon throughout the A/P/E corridor at day 0 is consistent with the AA-resolution sister CSV's K/R-at-A/P/E day-0 signal, and with electrostatic exit-tunnel slowing by basic residues. The codon-level resolution adds specificity: the signal localises on AAG, not AAA (see next paragraph).

**The Lys signal is codon-biased toward AAG over AAA (exploratory).** AAG and AAA encode the same amino acid (Lys), but at day-0 they move in opposite directions at the A site: A:AAA is in the concordant-depleted set (mean -0.39 across timepoints, 1/3 sig) while A:AAG is the second-largest enrichment row in the file. E:AAA is depleted at day 0 (-0.62, p_adj<0.05) while E:AAG is enriched at +0.74 — same site, same amino acid, opposite directions. This is exploratory rather than concrete: the AAA depletions sit at single-timepoint significance and the within-condition sister tests are the right place to corroborate whether the AAG-over-AAA bias is a perturbation x time pattern or a baseline codon-usage feature that survives the day-0 amplification. The within-condition binomial baselines are the obvious cross-check (does AAG at E sit at high baseline enrichment in every group?).

**Day-0 Arg enrichment is consistent across multiple synonyms (concrete).** Arg has 6 synonyms; CGC, AGA, and CGT all clear FDR<0.05 at day-0 in at least one site, with mean log2(OR) > +0.2 across timepoints (A:AGA +0.29 concordant, A:CGT +0.23 concordant, P:AGA +0.25 concordant, P:CGT +0.23 concordant). CGC at the P and E sites has the largest single-cell magnitudes (+0.95 and +0.92 respectively, both `rare-codon` flagged because BWM_k < 100). The cross-synonym coherence is the firm part: AGA and CGT carry the signal at high count; CGC tracks the same direction but its magnitude weight is reduced by the small-k flag. The mechanistic anchor (basic-residue electrostatic slowing in the exit tunnel) is the same as the AAG-Lys story; codon resolution shows that the BWM-control difference is distributed across multiple Arg codons rather than concentrated on one.

**The one FDR-significant direction flip needs codon-level scrutiny (exploratory).** E:AAG flips between day-0 (+0.75, sig) and day-5 (-0.23, sig); this is the only cell in the file with both opposite-sign rows clearing FDR<0.05, and it sits at high count (BWM_k = 866 at day 0). The flip pairs with the AA-sister's E:K flip (also 0 -> 5, also FDR-significant in both directions), and the within-condition Fisher d5_vs_d0 contrast is a natural triangulation point. Exploratory hypothesis: a transient day-5 BWM-specific depletion of AAG at the E site, on top of stable E:AAG enrichment in both conditions at baseline; concrete confirmation would require the within-condition binomial baselines and the within-condition Fisher contrast to cohere.

**The day-0 dominance of FDR hits is a power story, not biology (concrete).** 82 of 86 FDR<0.05 hits sit at day 0; day-5 and day-10 contribute 3 and 1 hit respectively. File-minimum p_adj spans 28 orders of magnitude over the same gradient (2.0e-30 -> 5.0e-3 -> 1.4e-1). The control:BWM N ratio is 4.55:1 at day 0, 0.94:1 at day 5, 1.27:1 at day 10 — the day-0 imbalance amplifies moderate effect sizes into extreme p magnitudes, while the same effect sizes at later timepoints stay below FDR. The `n-asymmetry-affects-power-not-direction` caveat applies directly: per-cell hit-count differences are power-modulated; the data do not support a "BWM-vs-control divergence is concentrated at day 0" reading without further power-corrected support from the within-condition contrasts.

**Many high-magnitude rare-codon depletions are noise, not biology (concrete).** The file's largest absolute |log2(OR)| values at p_adj<0.05 (A:TGT -1.84, A:TTT -1.52, A:AAT -1.50, A:TAT -1.35, P:ACA -2.00, E:ACG -2.29) are all rare-codon depletions with BWM_k under 40. Direction is consistent with the per-cell counts but magnitude is dominated by the small-k floor — these rows are exactly what the `rare-codon-low-count` caveat is designed to flag. They should not anchor any biological reading; the high-count concordant subset (A:AGA, P:AAG, P:GAA, P:AGA) is the right list to carry forward.

**What to carry forward.** *Concrete*: day-0 AAG-at-A/P/E Lys enrichment as a stable, multi-site, large-effect BWM signature; day-0 Arg enrichment carried across multiple synonyms (AGA, CGT primarily); the day-0/day-5/day-10 hit gradient is a power-story, not a time-course story. *Exploratory*: AAG vs AAA codon-usage divergence at day 0 (needs within-condition cross-check); the E:AAG day-0 -> day-5 sign flip (needs within-condition d5_vs_d0 Fisher to anchor). *Noise*: every rare-codon flagged row with BWM_k < 50 and |log2(OR)| > 1; the day-5/day-10 panels visually flat — at most a handful of single-cell hits, none above the high-count significance bar.

## Methods
Fisher's exact (two-sided) is run on a 2x2 contingency BWM_count vs control_count for each (timepoint, site, codon), with BH-FDR correction within each (timepoint, site) family of 61 sense codons. Effect column is `odds_ratio` (re-expressed as log2OR in this report's tables for symmetry with sister files); test statistic and exact p are returned by `scipy.stats.fisher_exact`. The p-correction column is `p_adj` (BH per (timepoint, site) family). The test answers "is the BWM-vs-control codon composition at this site different at this timepoint?". It does *not* answer "is each condition's per-codon enrichment vs background informative" (that is the `within_condition_binomial` family) and *not* "is the BWM-vs-control difference itself shifting across timepoints" (that would require an explicit between-timepoint contrast; see the `between_timepoint_wilcoxon` family for the within-condition variant of that question).

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **pseudorep.** 2x2 contingencies pool replicates by design; per-replicate variation is invisible to the statistic, so p-values are anti-conservative — read `odds_ratio` as the primary effect column and treat the smallest p-magnitudes as inflated.
- **large-N-Fisher-anticonservative.** With many thousands of pooled stall events and stalls correlated on shared motifs/transcripts, Fisher's effective N is smaller than `k_total`; treat tiny p magnitudes (`p_adj` < 1e-10) as inflated and rank by `log2_OR`.
- **imbalanced-N.** At day 0, control_total (27732) dwarfs BWM_total (6091); Fisher concentrates its information in the larger arm and inflates `p_adj` at moderate effects — read day-0 magnitudes against day-5/day-10 with the imbalance in mind.
- **bh-per-(timepoint,site).** BH is run independently within each of the 9 (timepoint, site) families of 61; rank within a (timepoint, site) cell, not across the full 549-test grid.
- **larger-bh-family.** Each per-(timepoint, site) BH family is 61 codons, ~3x larger than the AA sister's 20-AA families; AA-level signals can split across synonyms below the per-(timepoint, site) FDR threshold here. If a feature is sig at AA but absent here, that is an aggregation effect, not a contradiction.
- **rare-codon-low-count.** Many rare codons have `BWM_k` or `ctrl_k` in the single/double digits; OR estimates are unstable at small k and the file's largest |log2OR| values are all in this regime. Flagged rows should not anchor magnitude claims.
- **OR-direction-anchor.** OR > 1 = BWM-enriched, OR < 1 = BWM-depleted at this (timepoint, site, codon); stated here so downstream consumers do not invert the direction.
- **n-asymmetry-affects-power-not-direction.** The 28-OOM gap between min `p_adj` at day 0 (2.03e-30) and day 5 (4.99e-3) or day 10 (1.36e-1) is consistent with day 0's 4.55:1 control:BWM ratio amplifying moderate effects; per-cell hit counts are power-modulated, not direction-of-effect biology.

---

## Plots — individual

![Day-0 A-site codon BWM-vs-control volcano: x-axis log2(OR), y-axis -log10(FDR); triangles are FDR<0.05. AAG (basic blue) at +0.65 log2(OR), -log10(FDR) ~= 17 is the highest point; AAT (polar purple) and TAT (hydrophobic green) are the largest negative-direction labeled hits at log2(OR) ~= -1.5; many other triangles cluster between log2(OR) -2 and +1.](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/codon/individual/day_0_A_volcano.png)

![Day-0 P-site codon BWM-vs-control volcano: AAG (basic blue) at +0.69 log2(OR), -log10(FDR) ~= 18 is the highest point; ACA, AAT, AAA (polar/basic), TAT, GAA, CGC are labeled mid-magnitude hits; TTA (hydrophobic green) sits far left at log2(OR) ~= -2.8 below the FDR line (rare-codon).](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/codon/individual/day_0_P_volcano.png)

![Day-0 E-site codon BWM-vs-control volcano: AAG (basic blue) at +0.75 log2(OR), -log10(FDR) ~= 30 dominates the panel — by far the highest point in the file. AAT, AAA, CGC are labeled mid-magnitude triangles; a single hydrophobic point at log2(OR) ~= -10 (BWM_k = 0 ATA codon) sits at the left edge.](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/codon/individual/day_0_E_volcano.png)

![Day-5 A-site codon BWM-vs-control volcano: all points collapse near the origin within +/-0.5 log2(OR); no FDR<0.05 triangles. The flattest day-5 panel.](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/codon/individual/day_5_A_volcano.png)

![Day-5 P-site codon BWM-vs-control volcano: all points collapse near the origin; no FDR<0.05 triangles. A single basic-blue point (AAG) sits just at the FDR threshold but does not cross it.](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/codon/individual/day_5_P_volcano.png)

![Day-5 E-site codon BWM-vs-control volcano: three FDR<0.05 triangles labeled — AAG (basic blue) depleted at -0.23, GAG (acidic red) depleted at -0.30, CCA (neutral orange) enriched at +0.36. All three sit close to the origin with -log10(FDR) ~= 2-3.](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/codon/individual/day_5_E_volcano.png)

![Day-10 A-site codon BWM-vs-control volcano: no FDR<0.05 hits; all points fall below the FDR threshold within the +/-2 log2(OR) band — a flat panel.](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/codon/individual/day_10_A_volcano.png)

![Day-10 P-site codon BWM-vs-control volcano: GTC (hydrophobic green) at +0.56 log2(OR) is the single FDR<0.05 hit; a TTA point sits at log2(OR) ~= -10 at the left edge (control_k = 1 zero-cell row, ignored in headline rankings). All other points cluster near the origin.](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/codon/individual/day_10_P_volcano.png)

![Day-10 E-site codon BWM-vs-control volcano: no FDR<0.05 hits; all points fall below the FDR threshold within the +/-1 log2(OR) band — the flattest panel of the file.](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/codon/individual/day_10_E_volcano.png)
